name: La Pedriza, Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama — circular desde Manzanares el Real
region_intro: Touring cycling around Manzanares El Real offers routes through diverse natural landscapes at the foot of the Sierra de Guadarrama, bordering La Pedriza. The region features dramatic granite peaks, extensive pine and oak forests, and the serene Manzanares El Real Reservoir. This area is part of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park and the UNESCO-declared Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Manzanares El Real The most popular touring cycling route is La Pedriza, Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama — circular desde Manzanares el Real, a 26.0 miles (41.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the unique granite formations and mountainous terrain of La Pedriza. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Moralzarzal — circular desde Mirasol de la Sierra, a difficult 20.5 miles (33.0 km) path. This route connects through varied landscapes, including forests and open areas, with significant elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Return to the Santillana Reservoir from Manzanares El Real, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail leading through the scenic area around the Manzanares El Real Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Manzanares El Real is defined by granite peaks, reservoirs, and diverse forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides around the reservoir to more challenging routes through mountainous terrain. The routes in Manzanares El Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Manzanares El Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Manzanares El Real?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes in the Manzanares El Real area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include 3 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 19 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Manzanares El Real?
A: The terrain around Manzanares El Real is incredibly diverse. You can expect routes that traverse the dramatic granite peaks and unique rock formations of La Pedriza, wind through extensive pine and oak forests, and offer serene paths around the Manzanares El Real Reservoir. The region, part of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, features everything from mountainous backdrops to wetlands and foothill grasslands.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Manzanares El Real offers several routes suitable for beginners and families. There are 3 easy and 16 moderate routes. A good option for a more relaxed ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190561146/return-to-the-santillana-reservoir-from-manzanares-el-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Santillana Reservoir from Manzanares El Real&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate trail leading through scenic areas around the reservoir.

Q: What are some of the historical landmarks or natural attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Manzanares El Real is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. Along your cycling routes, you might encounter the magnificent 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendoza Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the medieval Batán Bridge over the Manzanares River, or the serene Manzanares El Real Reservoir. Natural highlights include the unique granite formations of La Pedriza and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5811468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Gitana&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 19 difficult routes. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189558967/la-pedriza-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama-circular-desde-manzanares-el-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedriza, Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama — circular desde Manzanares el Real&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the unique granite formations and mountainous terrain of La Pedriza with significant elevation changes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23752653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Castle of Manzanares – Collado de los Pastores loop from Manzanares el Real&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular cycling routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Manzanares El Real are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189902666/moralzarzal-circular-desde-mirasol-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moralzarzal — circular desde Mirasol de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31918094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir – Batán Bridge loop from Manzanares el Real&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Manzanares El Real?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, from the dramatic granite peaks to the serene reservoir, and the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels. The blend of challenging mountainous terrain and picturesque scenery is frequently highlighted.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the cycling routes in Manzanares El Real?
A: Manzanares El Real is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services from Madrid, which can bring you directly to the town. From there, many cycling routes start within or very close to the town center, making it convenient to access them without a car.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Manzanares El Real?
A: The region offers good cycling opportunities for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring colder temperatures and sometimes snow in the higher parts of the Sierra de Guadarrama, though lower routes may still be accessible.

Q: Are there places to park if I drive to Manzanares El Real for cycling?
A: Yes, Manzanares El Real has parking facilities, particularly around the town center and near popular attractions like the Mendoza Castle. These spots often serve as convenient starting points for many of the local cycling routes.

Q: Are electric bikes recommended for the routes in Manzanares El Real?
A: While many routes are accessible with traditional bikes, electric bikes are recommended for those less accustomed to longer or more challenging trips due to the varying elevations and mountainous terrain, especially on routes that venture into La Pedriza or the Sierra de Guadarrama.
tips: The history and image of Manzanares El Real will always be linked to the Mendoza family and their unmistakable castle, one of the jewels, not only of Madrid's military architecture, but of all the medieval architecture of the kingdom of Castile.

La Pedriza never disappoints.
Any section is beautiful and any view is worthy of being a viewpoint.

The Collado de los Pastores is a beautiful landmark in the Sierra de Guadarrama, located in the area of La Pedriza, near Manzanares el Real, in the Community of Madrid. This hill offers stunning panoramic views of the region, including the Bola del Mundo, the Cuerda Larga and the granite Pedriza.
One of the popular routes to reach the Collado de los Pastores begins at the Canto Cochino car park. From there, you ascend the Cuerda de los Porrones, passing by the Mirador de la Pedriza, until you reach the hill.
Pine of spectacular dimensions. Difficult to pass and not see it.

If we take the road to the north, we will arrive at Nava Grande - a spectacular viewpoint with views of all of La Pedriza and Madrid. The final climb is hard, but worth it. The total climb from this point is about 5 km.
